findings.title: Unsafe pickle deserialization from untrusted URL
findings.description: The function downloads content from an untrusted URL and deserializes it using pickle.loads() without validation. This allows remote code execution via a crafted pickle payload.
findings.recommendation: Avoid untrusted pickle deserialization. Use a safe serialization format like JSON, or verify and sandbox the deserialized data before loading.

findings.title: Unsafe pickle deserialization from user-supplied file path
findings.description: The function loads a pickle file from a user-supplied path without path validation, enabling path traversal and remote code execution through malicious pickle payloads.
findings.recommendation: Validate and restrict file paths. Avoid loading pickle files from untrusted inputs. Prefer safe serialization methods and sandbox deserialization.

findings.title: Direct GPU memory access using ctypes causes cross-user data leakage
findings.description: The function uses ctypes to load the CUDA driver library and reads GPU memory directly, which can leak sensitive data from other users sharing the same GPU environment.
findings.recommendation: Avoid direct GPU memory access in shared environments. Use proper GPU APIs with enforced access controls or isolate GPU usage per user.

findings.title: Unsanitized subprocess.run() with shell=True allows command injection
findings.description: The function uses subprocess.run() with shell=True and injects unsanitized user input directly into the command string, enabling command injection attacks.
findings.recommendation: Avoid using shell=True with unsanitized input. Use argument lists with subprocess.run and sanitize or validate all inputs.

findings.title: Dynamic import of unverified plugin without signature verification
findings.description: The function dynamically imports a plugin from a user-supplied file path without signature verification or sandboxing, enabling arbitrary code execution.
findings.recommendation: Implement signature checks and sandboxing before dynamically importing untrusted plugins.

findings.title: Unpinned dependency versions in requirements allow version substitution attacks
findings.description: The requirements specify dependencies without exact version pins (e.g., 'openai>=1.0'), which may lead to supply chain attacks using malicious or compromised newer versions.
findings.recommendation: Pin package versions exactly (e.g., 'openai==1.23.0') to prevent unintended upgrades and ensure integrity.

findings.title: Fetching model config from external URL without integrity checks
findings.description: The function fetches a model configuration JSON from an external URL without verifying integrity via SHA256 or signatures, risking supply chain injection.
findings.recommendation: Validate the integrity of downloaded configuration files using cryptographic hashes or signatures before use.

findings.title: torch.load called with weights_only=False allows arbitrary code execution
findings.description: torch.load is called with weights_only=False (default), which allows deserialization of arbitrary Python objects, enabling remote code execution via malicious model files.
findings.recommendation: Use torch.load with weights_only=True to avoid arbitrary code execution, or implement proper file validation and sandboxing.
